relations.note: Geometry math implements the box/badge composition.
Radius spec details the accent-shape box and badge.
Shadow is a mandatory attribute of the badge in the composition.
Fallback addresses client support for the absolute-positioned geometry.

quotes: A **light-blue accent-shape box** containing a short blue headline, with a **white icon badge that straddles the box's left edge**
**Box** ? Light Blue `#E6F0F9` fill, single accent shape.
**Text** ? Brand Blue `#00529b`, Nunito Sans weight 800, ~21px / 28px line-height, left-aligned, **vertically centered** to the box.
**Icon badge** ? a **White `#ffffff` rounded square** holding the line icon (Brand Blue stroke)
### [IMPORTANT] Icon Callout Box Rule
A **light-blue accent-shape box** containing a short blue headline
Used for supporting "proof point" statements (dosing convenience, time-to-effect, etc.).
the callout sits on the page/section background (usually White `#ffffff`)
**Box** ? Light Blue `#E6F0F9` fill, single accent shape.
**Text** ? Brand Blue `#00529b`, Nunito Sans weight 800, ~21px / 28px line-height, left-aligned, **vertically centered** to the box. Never let copy slide under the badge.
**Icon badge** ? a **White `#ffffff` rounded square** holding the line icon (Brand Blue stroke)

rules.intent: Preserve the signature icon-callout proof-point device and its color/type assignments.
rules.rule_text: The Icon Callout Box is a Light Blue (icon_callout.box.fill = #E6F0F9) single accent-shape box carrying a short Brand Blue (icon_callout.text.color = #00529b) headline in Nunito Sans weight 800 at ~21px / 28px line-height, left-aligned and vertically centered, sitting on the section background (usually White #ffffff) with section padding 16px 25px and box padding 26px 34px 26px 64px. A white (icon_callout.badge.fill = #ffffff) rounded-square icon badge holding a Brand Blue-stroke line icon straddles the box's left edge (half on the blue, half on the page background). It is used for supporting 'proof point' statements (dosing convenience, time-to-effect, etc.).

quotes: Let `W` = badge width (use **80px**, icon ?48px inside).
Inset the blue box from the left by **`W/2` = 40px**, and place the badge so its **center lands exactly on the box's left edge**
Vertical center via `top:50%` + `margin-top:?40px` (half the badge height).
the **left padding must clear the overhanging badge half plus a gap** (? `W/2 + 24px`)
Never let copy slide under the badge.
Let `W` = badge width (use **80px**, icon ?48px inside).
place the badge so its **center lands exactly on the box's left edge**
Vertical center via `top:50%` + `margin-top:?40px` (half the badge height).
Defaults: badge size `W = 80px`, inner icon `48px`.
keep the relationships � **inset = `W/2`**, **box left-padding = `W/2 + 24px`**, **badge `margin-top` = `?H/2`**

rules.intent: Lock the half-on/half-off badge placement math so the signature device renders exactly.
rules.rule_text: Badge geometry is exact: badge width W = 80px (inner icon 48px). The blue box is inset from the left by W/2 = 40px, and the badge is placed with its center landing exactly on the box's left edge (distance from badge center to box edge must be 0), giving a 50/50 split across the edge. Vertical centering is via top:50% plus margin-top:-40px (half the badge height). The box's left padding must clear the overhanging badge half plus a gap (≈ W/2 + 24px). Never let copy slide under the badge. If a different badge size is used, keep the relationships: inset = W/2, box left-padding = W/2 + 24px, badge margin-top = -H/2.
rules.snippets: <div style="position:absolute; left:0; top:50%; margin-top:-40px; width:80px; height:80px; background-color:#ffffff; border-radius:20px 4px 20px 4px; box-shadow:0 4px 10px rgba(1,30,69,0.18); text-align:center; line-height:80px;">

quotes: <div style="position:absolute; left:0; top:50%; margin-top:-40px; width:80px; height:80px; background-color:#ffffff; border-radius:20px 4px 20px 4px; box-shadow:0 4px 10px rgba(1,30,69,0.18); text-align:center; line-height:80px;">
box `border-radius: 40px 8px 40px 8px;` in `TL TR BR BL` order
The white badge **mirrors the same accent shape at smaller scale**, `border-radius: 20px 4px 20px 4px;`
**Corner radius (mandatory, never change):** box `border-radius: 40px 8px 40px 8px;` in `TL TR BR BL` order
`border-radius: 20px 4px 20px 4px;` (also 5:1, large TL+BR)
Never simplify either to a single uniform radius, swap the corners, or flip the diagonal.

rules.intent: Protect the signature asymmetric accent-shape radii on both box and badge.
rules.rule_text: Corner radius is mandatory and must never change: box border-radius: 40px 8px 40px 8px (TL TR BR BL) — large on top-left + bottom-right, near-point on top-right + bottom-left, a 5:1 ratio. The white badge mirrors the same accent shape at smaller scale, border-radius: 20px 4px 20px 4px (also 5:1, large TL+BR). Never simplify either to a single uniform radius, swap the corners, or flip the diagonal.

quotes: `box-shadow: 0 4px 10px rgba(1, 30, 69, 0.18);` (Dark Navy at low opacity)
**Drop shadow (mandatory):** the white icon badge **must carry a subtle drop shadow** so it lifts off the blue box � e.g. `box-shadow: 0 4px 10px rgba(1, 30, 69, 0.18);` (Dark Navy at low opacity).
This shadow is required on every instance; without it the badge looks flat and pasted-on.

rules.intent: Ensure the badge reads as lifted off the box on every instance.
rules.rule_text: The white icon badge must carry a subtle drop shadow so it lifts off the blue box: box-shadow: 0 4px 10px rgba(1, 30, 69, 0.18) (Dark Navy #011E45 at low opacity, 0.18). This shadow is required on every instance; without it the badge looks flat and pasted-on.

quotes: the 50/50 overhang relies on `position:absolute`, which Apple Mail / iOS / most web clients honor but **Outlook (desktop, Word engine) ignores**
the brand-safe option is to **pre-composite the white badge (with its shadow, accent radii, and icon) as a transparent PNG**

ship the live version above and accept the Outlook degrade (badge flush at the inner-left edge) � every other rule (Light Blue fill, `40px 8px 40px 8px` radius, blue 800 text, drop shadow) stays identical

rules.intent: Give a brand-safe rendering path for the absolute-positioned badge across email clients.
rules.rule_text: The 50/50 overhang relies on position:absolute, which Apple Mail / iOS / most web clients honor but Outlook (desktop, Word engine) ignores — there the badge drops to the top-left and the box won't be inset. For pixel-perfect fidelity across all clients, the brand-safe option is to pre-composite the white badge (with its shadow, accent radii, and icon) as a transparent PNG placed via the same absolute positioning, or bake the badge-on-edge treatment into the box's background image while keeping the text live. When that's not feasible, ship the live version and accept the Outlook degrade (badge flush at the inner-left edge) — every other rule (Light Blue fill, 40px 8px 40px 8px radius, blue 800 text, drop shadow) stays identical.
